rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MCPEEK VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant The Goodyear Tire Rubber Company'S
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiff's objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MCPEEK VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Jack Goldberg, Inc.'s renewed motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. The Court will allow defendant to make this motion pursuant to C.C.P. 1008(b). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Corporations Code sections 2011(a)(1)(A) and/or 2011(a)(2)(B) bar plaintiff's action against it. Mr. Goldberg's declaration is insufficient to demonstrate that Jack Goldberg, Inc. has no undistributed assets, including any insurance assets. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Roebbelen Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY BROWN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (pipe insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DALE STEIN et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Prohibiting The Destruction Of Relevant Evidence By Aerojet Rocketdyne, Inc. (Fka Aerojet General Corporation) And Gencorp, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 1, 2014 in Department 503, Line 5. Plaintiffs' motion for preliminary injunction prohibiting the destruction of relevant evidence by Aerojet Rocketdyne, Inc. and GenCorp, Inc. is continued on the court's own motion to May 7, 2014. No documents may be destroyed until the Court has ruled on this motion as it pertains to this case. = (503/TLJ)
